name: Dynaconf (Dynamic Configuration for Python)

auth.notes: Configuration library; secrets loaded from env or secrets managers

performance.notes: Config loaded at startup; negligible overhead

agent_readiness.notes: Feature-rich Python config library. settings.DATABASE_URL, settings.DEBUG. Sources: .toml, .yaml, .ini, .json, .env, environment variables, secrets. Layering: default → environment (development/staging/production) → env vars. @settings.validators for schema validation. settings.set() programmatic override. Flask/Django integration. Box-style access: settings.db.host. ENV_FOR_DYNACONF=production for environment selection. More features than python-dotenv but more complex.

gotchas: Global settings singleton can cause test isolation issues
Multiple config sources can make debugging surprising values
More complex than python-dotenv for simple apps
Magic merging behavior can be surprising
best_when: Projects needing layered config from multiple sources
Flask/Django apps wanting structured config beyond os.environ
Teams needing config validation with validators
avoid_when: Simple apps where python-dotenv is sufficient
Twelve-factor apps where environment variables are sufficient
summary: Dynaconf is a feature-rich Python configuration library. settings.KEY access. Multiple sources: .toml, .yaml, .env, env vars. Layered environments (development/staging/production) via ENV_FOR_DYNACONF. Config validation via validators. Flask/Django integration. Box-style nested access. More powerful than python-dotenv for complex multi-environment apps. Pydantic-based projects should use pydantic-settings instead.
